title: A misunderstanding about shoes in Istanbul
summary: A small story about getting a custom wrong on your first visit to someone's home.
topic: A misunderstanding about shoes in Istanbul
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a moment when you got a cultural custom wrong while travelling. Explain what happened, how the host reacted, and what you learned. Use past simple, past continuous, and 'should'.
hintStarters: On my first evening in…, my friend's family invited me to…
Without thinking, I walked into the living room and…
sampleText: On my first evening in Istanbul, my colleague Esra invited me to her family's flat for dinner. She had told me her mother was cooking something special, and I was nervous because I wanted to make a good impression. When she opened the door, I shook her father's hand and walked straight into the living room with my shoes on. The carpet was a beautiful dark red, and I left a small line of street dust across it without noticing. Esra's mother looked at the floor and then at her husband, but she did not say anything. Esra came over quickly and whispered, 'In our house, we always take our shoes off at the door.' I felt my face turn hot. I apologised four or five times, took off my shoes, and put on a pair of slippers near the doormat. Her mother smiled kindly and said it was nothing. While we were eating, her father told me that the carpet had been a wedding gift from his own mother thirty years ago. I should have remembered, because Esra had mentioned it at the office, but I had been thinking only about the food. After dinner, I helped clean the carpet, and we all laughed about it.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I wanted to make a good impression
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi muốn tạo ấn tượng tốt

sampleAnnotations.phrase: walked straight into the living room
left a small line of street dust
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: để lại một vệt bụi đường nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: we always take our shoes off at the door
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chúng tôi luôn cởi giày ở cửa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I felt my face turn hot
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi cảm thấy mặt nóng bừng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a pair of slippers near the doormat
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một đôi dép gần thảm chùi chân
đã từng là một món quà cưới
tất cả chúng tôi cười về chuyện đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Should have + past participle' for past regret
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Should have + V3' diễn tả tiếc nuối về một việc bạn đã không làm: 'I should have remembered.' = Lẽ ra tôi nên nhớ (nhưng tôi đã không nhớ).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Should have + past participle' expresses regret about something you did not do: 'I should have remembered.' = It was the right thing to do, but I didn't.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for what you were doing at the moment
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Past continuous mô tả việc đang diễn ra trong khi chuyện khác xảy ra: 'While we were eating, her father told me…' Past simple cho hành động ngắn 'told'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Past continuous describes what was in progress when another event happened: 'While we were eating, her father told me…' Past simple marks the short event 'told'.

sampleTranslationVi: Vào buổi tối đầu tiên của tôi ở Istanbul, đồng nghiệp Esra mời tôi đến căn hộ gia đình cô ấy dùng bữa tối. Cô ấy đã nói với tôi rằng mẹ cô đang nấu món gì đó đặc biệt, và tôi hồi hộp vì muốn tạo ấn tượng tốt. Khi cô ấy mở cửa, tôi bắt tay bố cô và đi thẳng vào phòng khách trong khi vẫn mang giày. Tấm thảm có màu đỏ sẫm rất đẹp, và tôi để lại một vệt bụi đường nhỏ trên đó mà không để ý. Mẹ Esra nhìn xuống sàn rồi nhìn chồng, nhưng bà không nói gì. Esra đi đến nhanh và thì thầm, 'Trong nhà tôi, chúng tôi luôn cởi giày ở cửa.' Tôi cảm thấy mặt nóng bừng. Tôi xin lỗi bốn năm lần, cởi giày, và đi vào một đôi dép gần thảm chùi chân. Mẹ cô mỉm cười dịu dàng và nói không có gì cả. Trong khi chúng tôi đang ăn, bố cô kể với tôi rằng tấm thảm đã từng là một món quà cưới từ mẹ của ông ấy ba mươi năm trước. Lẽ ra tôi nên nhớ, vì Esra đã nhắc đến ở văn phòng, nhưng tôi chỉ nghĩ đến đồ ăn. Sau bữa tối, tôi giúp lau tấm thảm, và tất cả chúng tôi cười về chuyện đó.

options: I should remembered that custom.
I should have remembered that custom.
I should to remember that custom.
I should remembering that custom.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Should have + past participle' for regret about the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple narration.
options: I shook her father's hand and walked straight into the living room.
I shake her father's hand and walked straight into the living room.
I have shaken her father's hand and walked straight into the living room.
I was shaking her father's hand and was walking straight into the living room.
mcQuestions.explanation: Two short past actions in sequence → past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple for two finished sequential actions.

options: feeling proud about yourself
feeling uncomfortable and ashamed because of a small mistake
feeling sleepy and bored
feeling cold and hungry
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Embarrassed' = feeling self-conscious about a small mistake.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural sentence with past continuous.
options: While we ate, her father told me about the carpet.
While we were eating, her father told me about the carpet.
While we are eating, her father told me about the carpet.
While we were ate, her father tells me about the carpet.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While' + past continuous; short event in past simple.

options: an unusual price
a habit or practice typical of a group of people
a job in a shop
a kind of food
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Custom' = a traditional way of doing things.

options: make a good impression
do a good impression
take a good impression
have a good impression on someone first time
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Make a good impression' is the standard collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct meaning of 'take off' (shoes).
options: to put on, wear
to remove
to clean
to buy new ones

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the right preposition: 'we take our shoes off ___ the door.'
options: at
in
on
from
mcQuestions.explanation: 'At the door' is the standard location phrase.

options: I apologised four or five times.
I apologised for four or five time.
I apologise four or five times yesterday.
I was apologise four or five times.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'My face turned hot' suggest?
options: I had a fever
I was embarrassed and blushing
I was cooking close to a fire
I drank a hot tea
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Face turned hot' is an idiom for blushing from embarrassment.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural ending sentence.
options: After dinner, we all was laughing about it.
After dinner, we all laughed about it.
After dinner, we all are laughing about it.
After dinner, we all has laughed about it.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'laughed' completes the past story.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small mistakes about local customs sometimes bring people closer rather than push them apart?
